metadata.title: Why a Quantum Packet Becomes Chirped as It Spreads
metadata.note: Clean build. Momentum distribution is conserved while position-momentum correlation develops. Removes underdetermined numerical seed and uses k_local=k0+C(t)(x-xc).

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. Release a Gaussian matter-wave packet and it broadens. Look inside its ca

beats.prompt: At release, take a Gaussian envelope multiplying a carrier with central wavenumber k zero. Its phase gradient is initially uniform across the envelope.
beats.label: At release, take a Gaussian envelope multiplying a carrier with central wavenumber k zero. Its phase

beats.prompt: The packet is a superposition of nearby Fourier components. Their amplitudes in momentum space remain fixed during free evolution; only their phases change.
beats.label: The packet is a superposition of nearby Fourier components. Their amplitudes in momentum space remai

beats.prompt: For a nonrelativistic free particle, omega equals h bar k squared over two m. Group velocity is h bar k over m, so larger k travels faster.
beats.label: For a nonrelativistic free particle, omega equals h bar k squared over two m. Group velocity is h ba

beats.prompt: As time passes, high-k components become enriched toward the leading side while low-k components lag toward the trailing side.
beats.label: As time passes, high-k components become enriched toward the leading side while low-k components lag

beats.prompt: This sorting is a correlation, not a change in the overall momentum histogram. The same k values remain, but position now tells you something about which ones d
beats.label: This sorting is a correlation, not a change in the overall momentum histogram. The same k values rem

beats.prompt: The evolved Gaussian carries a quadratic phase across its envelope. Differentiate that phase with position and the local wavenumber becomes a linear ramp.
beats.label: The evolved Gaussian carries a quadratic phase across its envelope. Differentiate that phase with po

beats.prompt: Write it schematically as k local equals k zero plus C of t times x minus x center. For positive time, C is positive.
beats.label: Write it schematically as k local equals k zero plus C of t times x minus x center. For positive tim

beats.prompt: So behind the center, local k is lower and the wavelength is longer. Ahead of the center, local k is higher and the wavelength is shorter.
beats.label: So behind the center, local k is lower and the wavelength is longer. Ahead of the center, local k is

beats.prompt: At the far tails, amplitude is tiny, so a local wavelength there has little experimental weight. The visible chirp matters where the envelope remains appreciabl
beats.label: At the far tails, amplitude is tiny, so a local wavelength there has little experimental weight. The

beats.prompt: Reverse the evolution mathematically and the phase correlation reverses. Interactions or lenses can also add, remove, or invert chirp, but free spreading create
beats.label: Reverse the evolution mathematically and the phase correlation reverses. Interactions or lenses can

beats.prompt: The packet does not manufacture momentum. Quadratic dispersion sorts its existing components in space, turning a uniform phase slope into a chirped one.
beats.label: The packet does not manufacture momentum. Quadratic dispersion sorts its existing components in spac
Your turn. If the local phase is k zero x plus b x squared, differentiate it and identify the local
Free dispersion keeps the momenta and rearranges where they appear.
